title: trichilia verrucosa
intro: Trichilia verrucosa, also known as the rough trichilia or African bitter orange, is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae that is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Trichilia verrucosa include the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its use as an antiseptic and astringent. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
